description: I was driving westbound on I-76 when I first saw the Snowy Owl.  I exited and stopped for a while, taking some photos.  The pale, large, and bulky appearance of this species is distinctive.  The dark barring on the undersides and wing coverts, and the dark spots on the forehead, are typical for an immature bird.  The bird was resting the entire time I watched it, only occasionally turning its head or opening its eyes.  No calls could be heard above the loud traffic noise.
